page: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Falsv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server has no local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core score in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12288.15 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6710.0. Memory performance presents a notable tradeoff: while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read bandwidth is weak, falling in the bottom 25% at 36.2 GB/sec, which is also reflected in a weak PassMark memory score. Text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 25%.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and high single-core performance, this server is structured for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. Qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the low memory-to-CPU ratio, which reduces memory overhead costs for CPU-heavy workloads. However, the 8.0 GB memory limit and weak uncached memory bandwidth represent significant tradeoffs for memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, text processing, and small-scale model inference, while it is less suitable for large database operations or memory-heavy datasets.
description: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the Falsv6 family, featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, representing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance in the top 10% and strong Geekbench single-core results. However, memory bandwidth for uncached large blocks is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, low-memory tasks by avoiding the premium of excess RAM. The server is suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight web serving, but is constrained for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 3.7 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance, suited for low-memory compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4als_v6 is a 4-vCPU compute-optimized server with an AMD EPYC processor, 8.0 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high single-thread performance for memory-lean workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10%
Weak uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 36.2 GB/sec
Best suited for single-threaded processing, text processing, and lightweight web serving
